Title: SERVER Alert - Attention - Kss-hq-vbr-01: (172.16.32.12) - Device not responding: Probably down or busy
Notes: As per the alert, we checked and found that the VM was showing as down on the portals. We then connected to the support machine and logged into the ESXi host, where the VM was actually up but had an issue with the network adapter. We disabled and re-enabled the adapter, then restarted the ScreenConnect services. The server is now up and running fine on the portals

Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version 11.4.2528.25.W updated:- KABRESRIGHT and  KABSUPPORT
Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.4.2528.25.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine (KABRESRIGHT and  KABSUPPORT) from Manage Engine, Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

Title: My Remote version 25.9.5.9483 updated on machine KABDT001,KABDT002,KABDT003,KABDT005
Notes:
Review the my remote portal, and found  the machine KABDT001,KABDT002,KABDT003,KABDT005
running with old version of My Remote. Tried to reinstall the agent from my remote portal, but not succeeded. Tried to restart the service and reinstall again, but unable to update it. So connected to the machine from manage engine  .
Uninstalled the old version and reinstalled the latest version 25.9.5.9483. After that refresh the machine from the portal. It was updated to latest
version of My Remote. Hence closing this ticket.

Title: Failed patches updated on machine VMKABDC1, KABSUPPORT
Notes:
Worked on the ticket. Login to the manage engine portal and checked failed or missing patches patches for the below mentioned machine then download and installed

the failed patches manually which are applicable to the computer. Then reboot the machine and scan the machine for installed patches.

The failed patches are updated successfully and machine are in healthy state.

 

Machine : VMKABDC1, KABSUPPORT

 

Patch installed:

Patch ID Patch Name

354976 Mozilla Firefox ESR (115) (x64) (115.32.0)

355222 VMware tools 13 (x64) (13.0.10)

Title: KSS-HQ-VBR-01 server was showing offline in my remote
Notes: While monitoring the Veeam Backups for KSS found KSS-HQ-VBR-01 server was showing offline in my remote. So checked the server connectivity, it was showing up. So connected to the server from manage engine, tried to restart the screen connect services, but nothing happened. So  reinstalled the my remote agent, now the server s showing up in my remote.

Title: Crowdstrike latest version(7.33.20505.0)updated on machine :- KABDT001, KABDT002, KSS-HQ-VBR-01
Notes: Connected to the machines (KABDT001, KABDT002, KSS-HQ-VBR-01) and discovered they were running an older version of CrowdStrike. Attempted to push the sensor from the portal, but the process was unsuccessful.so connect to the system through MYrmm and uninstalled the older version, downloaded, and installed the latest sensor version (7.33.20505.0) on these machines. Verified the update through the portal. Hence, closing this ticket.

Notes: Firewall Configuration backup has been done for KSS.

Title: Communication issue detected on Distribution Server VMKABDC01
Notes: While checking devices status on manage engine admi console for client, distribution server had issue in syncing and it keep revolve and keeps trying to sync with no changes, Connect on VMKABDC01 server tried to restart Manage engine DS service restart but that doesn't work, From manage engine remove VMKABDC01 as AD connector and deleted it as DS server download package  and install new package , Once installed syncing is working fine now verified from the Mange engine portal.

Title: System pending for updates after APD
Notes:
For KSS client:

 

Login in manage engine portal and checked  machines . Found below mentioned server pending for reboot after APD. So login into the servers, and restart the server.

After reboot login into the server, and checked required services are running. Then login again in manage engine portal and scan the machines for installed patches in APD. After scan  the machines are now in healthy systems.

For system installed the patches and now the systems are in healthy state.
